queries.answer:
Assuming you mean **Cardiff, Wales**: my pick would be **Jones The Barber** in **Castle Arcade**.

**Best overall:** **Jones The Barber** — 45 Castle Arcade, Cardiff. It’s a traditional barbershop in the city centre, offers cuts/trims and hot towel shaves, and is consistently recommended locally; Barberhead lists it at **4.7/5 from 195 reviews**, and recent Cardiff Reddit threads also mention Jones repeatedly for city-centre barbering. ([jonesthebarber.com](https://www.jonesthebarber.com/?utm_source=openai))

**Also very strong:** **Lazarou Cardiff Castle** — Duke Street, opposite the Castle. It’s a bigger salon/barber setup with a huge review base on Fresha — **4.9 with 10,000+ reviews** — so it’s a safe bet if you want easy booking and lots of availability. ([fresha.com](https://www.fresha.com/a/lazarou-cardiff-castle-hair-salon-extensions-barbers-cardiff-12-duke-street-tzsq6klh?utm_source=openai))

**If you’re after fades / high ratings:** check **Alpha Omega Grooming**, **Richy’s Barbershop**, or **Crooks Barber Shop** — Barberhead lists these among Cardiff’s highly rated options, each showing **5.0** ratings with dozens of reviews. ([barberhead.com](https://barberhead.com/cardiff?utm_source=openai))

**Quick recommendation:**  
- **Classic cut / beard / city centre:** Jones The Barber  
- **Most review-backed / easy booking:** Lazarou Cardiff Castle  
- **Fades:** Alpha Omega, Richy’s, or Crooks  

I’d book ahead rather than walk in, especially Friday/Saturday.

Assuming you mean **Cardiff, Wales**, my top pick is:

**Lazarou Barbers & Men’s Hair — Churchill Way**  
- **Rating:** 5.0 on Fresha with **6,729 reviews**  
- **Location:** 12a & 12B Churchill Way, Cardiff city centre  
- **Why pick it:** Very high review count + perfect rating, central location, and they specifically offer barbering/men’s styling. ([fresha.com](https://www.fresha.com/en-GB/a/lazarou-barbers-mens-hair-city-centre-churchill-way-cardiff-cardiff-churchill-way-vcho4oxc?utm_source=openai))

Good alternatives:

1. **Sage Hair Care — Fairwater**  
   **5.0 with 928 reviews** on Fresha; strong option if you want a highly rated local barber with easier parking outside the city centre. ([fresha.com](https://www.fresha.com/a/sage-hair-care-fairwater-cardiff-cardiff-unit-8-strawfam-offices-upgdylza?utm_source=openai))

2. **Lazarou Cardiff Castle**  
   **4.9 with 15,375 reviews** on Fresha; big review volume and very central near Cardiff Castle, though it’s more of a salon/barber mix. ([fresha.com](https://www.fresha.com/lp/en/gb-cardiff?utm_source=openai))

3. **Jones The Barber — Castle Arcade**  
   **4.7 from 195 reviews** and often recommended for a classic city-centre barber option. ([barberhead.com](https://barberhead.com/cardiff/jones-the-barber?utm_source=openai))

If you want the safest “top-rated” choice: **book Lazarou Churchill Way**.

Assuming you mean **Cardiff, Wales**: I’d recommend **Capello Barbers** as the safest all-round pick — especially if you want a modern cut/fade, beard work, or a wet shave. They have Cardiff branches in **City Centre, Cathays, and Pontcanna**, have been around since **1994**, take bookings, and also welcome walk-ins. ([capellobarbers.co.uk](https://www.capellobarbers.co.uk/))

**If you’re right in the city centre**, also consider **Lazarou Barbers Cardiff Castle** at **1 Duke Street Arcade** — good for classic cuts, fades, restyles, and consultations; it’s very central near Cardiff Castle. ([lazarouhairsalons.co.uk](https://www.lazarouhairsalons.co.uk/post/lazaroubarberscardiffcastle))

**Other good options by area/style:**
- **Morgan & Co, Cathays** — good if you want a barber who doesn’t rush; they note appointments can run from 15 minutes to 1 hr 15 mins, and they offer a student discount Monday–Friday. ([mandcobarbers.co.uk](https://www.mandcobarbers.co.uk/))  
- **The Barberhood, Butetown/Cardiff Bay side** — straightforward pricing, adult cut from **£25**, beard trims, hot towel shaves, and walk-ins or appointments. ([thebarberhood.co.uk](https://www.thebarberhood.co.uk/))  
- **Office Barbershop** — friendly, expert setup with locations on **Cowbridge Road East** and **The Maltings**. ([theofficebarbershop.com](https://www.theofficebarbershop.com/))

**My pick:** book **Capello City Centre** if you want convenient and reliable; book **Lazarou Duke Street Arcade** if you’re near the Castle and want a more established Cardiff-name barbershop.

queries.query: who are the best barber  in Cardiff
queries.answer:
Here are some strong Cardiff barber options I’d shortlist, depending on what you want:

1. **Lazarou Barbers – Cardiff Castle / Duke Street Arcade**  
   Best all-round city-centre choice. A 2026 Cardiff barbers guide ranks the Duke Street Arcade branch #1 and highlights it for sharp fades, beard trims and traditional hot towel shaves. Lazarou is also a long-running South Wales hair/barbering group, established since 1966. ([barbersofcardiff.co.uk](https://www.barbersofcardiff.co.uk/))

2. **Capello Barbers – City Centre, Cathays, Pontcanna**  
   Good if you want a modern barber with multiple Cardiff locations. They offer haircuts, fades, beard trims, wet shaves and kids’ cuts; bookings are advised but walk-ins are welcome. ([capellobarbers.co.uk](https://www.capellobarbers.co.uk/))

3. **Gents of Brooklyn – Cardiff City Centre**  
   Best if you want a more “premium/luxury” barber experience. Their Cardiff shop is on **42 Charles Street**, and they focus on the full experience as well as the haircut. ([gentsofbrooklyn.com](https://www.gentsofbrooklyn.com/))

4. **Jones The Barber – Castle Arcade**  
   Good traditional gentleman’s barber in the city centre. Based at **45 Castle Arcade**, it has lots of customer praise for being friendly, relaxed and good value. ([jonesthebarber.com](https://www.jonesthebarber.com/reviews))

5. **The Office Barbershop – Canton / East Tyndall Street**  
   Good for a friendly, appointment-based barber. They list services for men’s haircuts and have locations at **57 Cowbridge Road East** and **The Maltings, East Tyndall Street**. ([theofficebarbershop.com](https://www.theofficebarbershop.com/))

6. **Morgan & Co Barbershop – Cathays**  
   Good for Cathays / student area. They’re at **111 Woodville Road**, and describe themselves as a traditional barbershop focused on not rushing appointments. ([mandcobarbers.co.uk](https://www.mandcobarbers.co.uk/))

7. **Sunny’s Barbers – Ely / Grand Avenue**  
   Good walk-in option outside the centre. They welcome walk-ins daily, were established in 2008, and are at **357 Grand Avenue, Cardiff CF5 4RD**. ([sunnybarbers.com](https://www.sunnybarbers.com/))

**My top pick:** If you’re in the city centre, try **Lazarou Cardiff Castle** or **Capello City Centre**. If you want something more premium, try **Gents of Brooklyn**.

If you mean **Cardiff, Wales**, the most “popular” barber by review volume/popularity listings appears to be **Jones The Barber** in **Castle Arcade** — it’s listed with **4.7★ from 195 reviews** and the most upvotes on Barberhead’s Cardiff ranking. ([barberhead.com](https://barberhead.com/cardiff/jones-the-barber?utm_source=openai))

**Top pick:**  
**Jones The Barber**  
📍 45 Castle Arcade, Cardiff  
Good choice if you want a central Cardiff barber with lots of customer feedback.

A strong alternative if you prefer the **highest rating** rather than most reviews:  
**Alpha Omega Grooming**  
📍 36 Caerau Lane, Cardiff  
Listed at **5.0★ from 86 reviews**. ([barberhead.com](https://barberhead.com/cardiff/alpha-omega-grooming?utm_source=openai))

Other well-rated options include **Richy’s Barbershop** — **5.0★ from 62 reviews** — and **Crooks Barber Shop** — **5.0★ from 48 reviews**. ([barberhead.com](https://barberhead.com/cardiff/richy-s-barbershop?utm_source=openai))

If you mean **Cardiff, Wales**, here are some affordable options:

1. **Barber School Wales / Capello Barbers** — **free haircuts** by supervised trainee barbers; you need to book online and allow about **1–1.5 hours**. There may be a **£5 holding/no-show fee**. ([capellobarbers.co.uk](https://www.capellobarbers.co.uk/free-haircuts-1))  
2. **Severn Barbers** — **84 Severn Rd, CF11 9EA**. Listed prices include **gents dry cut £8**, clipper cut £7, senior cut £5, child cut £5. ([yell.com](https://www.yell.com/biz/severn-barbers-cardiff-7579074/))  
3. **Lamos Barbershop** — **47a Wilson Road, Cardiff**. Listed prices include **men’s cut £8.50**, fade £10, beard trim £2.50. ([bizseek.co.uk](https://www.bizseek.co.uk/lamos-barbershop-07582-397691))  
4. **Jones The Barber** — **45 Castle Arcade, city centre**. More mid-range but still reasonable: **cut £22**, buzz cut £14, NUS/student £19.50 excluding skin fades. ([jonesthebarber.com](https://www.jonesthebarber.com/services))  
5. **The Barberhood** — **16 West Bute Street, Butetown**. Adult cut from **£25**, beard trim £5, senior cut £15. ([thebarberhood.co.uk](https://www.thebarberhood.co.uk/))  

**Best cheapest pick:** Severn Barbers or Lamos.  
**Best if you want near-free:** Barber School Wales / Capello trainee appointments. Call or check booking first, as prices can change.

Here are a few **barbers in Cardiff city centre**:

1. **Capello Barbers – St Mary Street**  
   2nd Floor, 26–27 St Mary St, CF10 1AB — above Tesco.  
   Offers online booking; open Mon–Sat, closed Sunday. ([capellobarbers.co.uk](https://www.capellobarbers.co.uk/cardiff-city-centre?utm_source=openai))

2. **Bauhaus Barbers – The Friary**  
   The Friary, Cardiff CF10 3FA — between Queen Street and Greyfriars Road.  
   Open Mon–Sat, closed Sunday; online booking available. ([hairbauhaus.com](https://www.hairbauhaus.com/the-friary/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Medusa Barber Shop – Queen’s Arcade / St David’s Centre**  
   Queen St, Queen’s Arcade, St David’s Centre, CF10 2BY.  
   Listed as open 7 days, including Sunday. ([ratingsplus.co.uk](https://ratingsplus.co.uk/city/cardiff/barbers/medusa-barber-shop-cardiff-city-centre?utm_source=openai))

4. **Bonnie & Clyde Barber Shop – Queen Street**  
   99 Queen St, Cardiff CF10 2BG.  
   Offers haircuts and beard trims. ([poyst.com](https://www.poyst.com/business/bonnie-clyde-barber-shop-city-centre-cardiff?utm_source=openai))

If you want one **right in the middle of town**, I’d try **Capello on St Mary Street** or **Bauhaus at The Friary**.

queries.query: where can I find a good barber  in Cardiff
queries.answer:
If you mean **Cardiff, Wales**, here are a few solid places to start:

1. **Capello Barbers** — good all-round choice, with Cardiff locations in **City Centre, Cathays, and Pontcanna**. They offer haircuts, beard services, wet shaves, online booking, and say walk-ins are welcome though bookings are advised. ([capellobarbers.co.uk](https://www.capellobarbers.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))

2. **Morgan & Co Barbershop** — in **Cathays**, good if you want a more appointment-based, unrushed cut; they list services like haircut & style, skin fade, beard sculpt, and wash/restyle. ([mandcobarbers.co.uk](https://www.mandcobarbers.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Lazarou Barbers / Lazarou Hair Group** — long-running Cardiff/South Wales name, with barbering and salon services; a local Cardiff guide lists the Duke Street Arcade branch near Cardiff Castle highly. ([barbersofcardiff.co.uk](https://www.barbersofcardiff.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Severn Barbers** — central Cardiff option for skin fades, scissor cuts, beard styling, and grooming. ([severnbarbers.co.uk](https://severnbarbers.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))

5. **The Office Barbershop** — on **Cowbridge Road East**, describes itself as a friendly, expert barbershop in Cardiff. ([theofficebarbershop.com](https://www.theofficebarbershop.com/?utm_source=openai))

6. **Sunny’s Barbers** — based in **CF5**, useful if you’re in west Cardiff rather than the city centre. ([sunnybarbers.com](https://www.sunnybarbers.com/?utm_source=openai))

For quick checking, I’d search the shop name on **Google Maps** and look at recent photos/reviews, especially for the specific style you want — skin fade, beard trim, scissor cut, Afro hair, long hair, etc.

If you tell me **which part of Cardiff you’re in** and the style you want, I can narrow it down to the best 2–3 nearby.

If you mean **a barber in Cardiff**, here are a few good options:

- **Gents of Brooklyn – Cardiff City Centre**  
  42 Charles St, St David’s Centre, CF10 2GE — haircuts from **£25**, beard trims from **£10**; open Mon–Fri 9am–7pm, Sat 9am–3pm. ([gentsofbrooklyn.com](https://www.gentsofbrooklyn.com/pages/gents-of-brooklyn-cardiff-city-centre?utm_source=openai))

- **Bauhaus Barbers – The Friary**  
  The Friary, Cardiff CF10 3FA — city-centre barber; open Mon–Fri mostly 10am–6pm, Thu until 8pm, Sat 9am–5pm. ([hairbauhaus.com](https://www.hairbauhaus.com/the-friary/?utm_source=openai))

- **Lazarou Barbers – Cardiff Castle / Duke Street Arcade**  
  1 Duke Street Arcade, CF10 1AZ — long-running Cardiff option near the castle; listed with a 4.4 rating on Poyst. ([poyst.com](https://www.poyst.com/business/lazarou-barbers-cardiff-castle?utm_source=openai))

- **Capello Barbers – St Mary Street**  
  2nd floor, 26–27 St Mary St, CF10 1AB — central location; listed opening hours include Fri 8am–6pm and Sat 8am–1pm. ([find-open.co.uk](https://find-open.co.uk/cardiff/capello-barbers-cardiff-city-centre-3873950?utm_source=openai))

- **Babylon Barbers – City Road**  
  128 City Rd, CF24 3DR — open late most days, listed as closing 10pm. ([bizseek.co.uk](https://www.bizseek.co.uk/babylon-barbers-029-2132-0830?utm_source=openai))

If you want, tell me your area — **city centre, Cathays, Roath, Cardiff Bay, Canton, etc.** — and whether you want **cheap, skin fade, beard trim, Afro hair, walk-in, or open today**, and I’ll narrow it down.

queries.query: is there a good barber  in Cardiff
queries.answer:
Assuming you mean **Cardiff, Wales** — yes. A few good options depending on what you need:

- **Sage Hair Care** — strong pick if you want a more premium/experienced barber; they describe themselves as one of Cardiff’s highest-rated barbershops, and founder Tom Sage has award recognition in UK/Welsh barbering. ([sagehaircare.com](https://sagehaircare.com/?utm_source=openai))  
- **Morgan & Co Barbershop** — good for a proper appointment-based cut rather than a rushed walk-in; based in **Cathays**, near Cardiff city centre, and offers haircuts, skin fades, beard sculpting, and restyles. ([mandcobarbers.co.uk](https://www.mandcobarbers.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))  
- **Lazarou Barbers Cardiff Castle** — convenient if you’re in the **city centre**; it’s at **1 Duke Street Arcade** near Cardiff Castle and is part of the long-running Lazarou name in Cardiff. ([barberspot.net](https://barberspot.net/en-gb/i/2634-lazarou-barbers-cardiff-castle/?utm_source=openai))  
- **Crooks Barber Shop** — worth considering if you’re near **Fairoak Road**; Barberhead lists it as 5/5 from 48 verified reviews. ([barberhead.com](https://barberhead.com/cardiff/crooks-barber-shop?utm_source=openai))  
- **Gents of Brooklyn** — another Cardiff barber brand to check if you want a more “luxury” barber/grooming vibe. ([gentsofbrooklyn.com](https://www.gentsofbrooklyn.com/?utm_source=openai))  

If you want my best quick suggestion: **Morgan & Co** for a reliable modern cut/skin fade, or **Lazarou Cardiff Castle** if you need somewhere central. If you tell me your area of Cardiff and whether you want a skin fade, beard trim, Afro/curly hair, or long-hair tidy-up, I can narrow it down.
